codeReview.summary: The feature largely matches the requested persistence and UI behavior, but it can record false-positive successful snippet runs because history is persisted after any transcript change rather than evidence that the dispatched snippet actually reached the remote side.

issues.description: Successful runs are recorded after any transcript delta, not after proof that the just-dispatched payload reached the remote side. `dispatchIntoCurrentSession()` snapshots the transcript, sends the snippet, then treats `state.transcript != transcriptBeforeDispatch` as success and immediately persists history with `markExecuted()`. Unrelated remote output arriving during that 5-second window would incorrectly create a recent-history entry and update `lastRunAt`, violating the architecture requirement that success be recorded only once the session transcript confirms the snippet run itself reached the remote side.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, architecture docs, and commit `a200cd22f567817d2e6cc6b0478d4201813c375b`. I found one blocking correctness issue: snippet history persistence relies on any transcript mutation as proof of success, so unrelated remote output can produce false successful history entries.
